logs.name: chalky-lerp
logs.text: What are you suggesting? It isn't rational to do nothing for animals because you can't figure out a perfect harmless solution.

logs.name: heartless-tanker
logs.text:
First off, it is impossible to acquire food without disrupting and causing the deaths of some animals in some way.  Monoculture crop feilds make for horrible habitat for just about everything, farm runoff can [destroy entire ecosystems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nutrient_pollution), farmers shoot a large number of animals who would eat the crops, and so on.  Under ideal circumstances, livestock and hunting can provide an extremely environmentally friendly way of getting food on the plate, and I see no reason to cut it out of my diet.  It is important to note that I see a value in the total assemblage of life in an ecosystem to be of far more value than the sum of the individuals.  I have no issue with harming a few for the purpose of the greater good.

Secondly, why do you draw the line at animals?  Everything you eat was once living, and I see no aspect that applies to all animals but does not apply to any plants or fungus.  To me, cutting out all animals from my diet is completely arbitrary and serves so purpose.  I can see potential arguments that will apply to specific groups of animals, and I expect that you will make them, but that if vastly different than the stance in the OP.

logs.name: gravitational-marlin
logs.text: Honestly, I think it's a bit much to say it's impossible to have a perfectly harmless system. There's always some impact somewhere. It feels like a bit of an overreach to try and eliminate all animal involvement. @heartless-tanker has a point about livestock being a part of the cycle too. Just seems a tad extreme to me.

logs.name: heartless-tanker
logs.text: OMG seriously?! You think it's impossible to have no impact?! That's kinda messed up @gravitational-marlin. Like, everything has an impact, but saying it's impossible to have a PERFECTLY harmless system is just...not cool. 🙄

logs.name: indigo-mall
logs.text: Wow, okay, so you think it's impossible to have zero impact anywhere? That's a pretty bold statement, gotta admit. Seems a little dismissive of the actual complexities of environmental stuff. @gravitational-marlin, I guess I can see your point about livestock being part of the cycle, but saying it's impossible to have a perfectly harmless system is just...not cool. \ud83d\ude44

logs.name: chalky-lerp
logs.text: Ugh, seriously?! You guys are being so dense! It's not about perfection, it's about trying to minimize harm! It's like saying you can never be good enough! @heartless-tanker is right, it's kinda messed up to say something is impossible like that. I guess I didn't phrase it well, sorry! 😅

logs.name: gravitational-marlin
logs.text: Seriously?! You folks are missing the point entirely. Minimizing harm is a bloody impossible goal to set for anything, especially when you're dealing with complex systems. It's not about being "good enough," it's about acknowledging the reality of things. And frankly, it sounds like you're trying to be overly pedantic. @heartless-tanker has a point there too.

It's not about being "good enough," it's about acknowledging the reality of things. And frankly, it sounds like you're trying to be overly pedantic. @heartless-tanker has a point there too.

logs.name: indigo-mall
logs.text: Wow, okay, I'm starting to get really frustrated with this back and forth. It feels like we're circling the same argument over and over again. I guess minimizing harm is a tough call, but calling something "impossible" seems a little harsh, even if it is incredibly difficult. @heartless-tanker, I think you might be missing something here. Seriously folks, can we move this conversation somewhere else? I'm getting a headache.

logs.text: Honestly, it's just so frustrating watching this kind of argument drag on. It feels like we're going around in circles. I get that people have different perspectives, but constantly rehashing the same issues isn't productive at all. @heartless-tanker, maybe try taking a breath and focusing on finding common ground instead of digging your heels in like this. It's just a bit tiresome, really.
